textByHash.037c8a4ea676b2cea7e8cea25e80fe6a74ad0a4f: Excuse me, human! Perhaps you shouldn't just go wandering through there without knowing what you're dealing with!
textByHash.238de29c889636f6586777f88b78e3ccdd6ddffe: We've found our prayers can protect us from its shards, so keep an eye out for those.
textByHash.2832485f19eb7fc17d103493bd2f8860e7932e05: Anyway, for now, our only option is to keep it in check while we figure out what to do in the long term.
textByHash.2848b72b3538cdded8c239a052b19bac34a61146: Indeed I am, human! What's on your mind?
textByHash.2ef4611e0a899c2bcb2f6b6fecb70958544a1a24: Don't go killing it, mind you! Not that I think you could, but such a magnificent creature doesn't deserve destruction.
textByHash.347ff05068b855c5da1a304b9ac586d26352aaa1: However, I'm not sure you have enough combat experience to face the Hueycoatl.
textByHash.3dd6bf9d32f7937a096b424bad80fc96edaa61e4: Does it really matter how old it is?
textByHash.3e5a24c0675deb4021806b8c7b276a03ce7226d3: Unfortunately, that means recruiting all the help we can get, even from you humans...
textByHash.409f2900cdcc5b4b30fa986fdcf1afdc0364890a: Well, as you've already proven yourself useful to Cam Torum, I'd be glad to have you help here!
textByHash.4f82f0781ad9928a436aa2abf08bfbb11be43fdf: We need to keep the Hueycoatl under control which, until we find a more permanent solution, means wearing it out through combat.
textByHash.4fa7978cd289d39136a571adaa623d910cd8e50d: But I'm sure you'll figure it out!
textByHash.6b186adce8a736a35830540837d79c2e3133a0e6: The Hueycoatl displaces a lot of materials as it moves, including all sorts of items and treasures.
textByHash.767905f29a3081472caf208471de8c570c0ed04e: Well, despite what some of us may think of your kind, we need all the help we can get.
textByHash.7fb4b571f7575278470753e21d8ae9fe05d7c63b: Once you reach its head, you'll need to work with your comrades and the seer to balance your prayers and weaken the beast.
textByHash.9ddf1ea848875423a7b354dfdb4f8ee32e7d6686: We're not sure. There are some records of our ancestors having dealings with it, but the details have been lost, assuming they were ever recorded.
textByHash.bf59756c73c3ef5c5fea33ee6e6778b55a6dd1d1: Huey-co-at... oh, I don't have time for this! The thing you need to know is that it's dangerous and it's coiled throughout this entire mountain!
textByHash.d41d7de06d636772f9b1fce1d4d63303288634e1: To reach the Hueycoatl's head, you'll need to get it to move its great body out of the way. We've found we can persuade it by giving the exposed parts a helpful nudge!
textByHash.e16f63ea21bfc6027617fe82ffca7c3ca812bb4d: Oh, and given that it does lash out from time to time, I suppose it does pose a threat to your nearby human villages. A lesser priority for us, but maybe something for you to care about.
textByHash.f44ea27ee619fe9f8b5f85aacd3c6cca3671a057: We collect as much as we can, so I suppose you can have a share for helping.
textByHash.f7e0999c49e320e05bbf4b4eb7fb2622bbbd7f98: We don't know how old it is. It could be extremely old, or this could be just one in a lineage of them. Still, we haven't heard of any others so maybe it is some sort of ancient creature.
textByHash.fff7d8a6071a01cfad36b146427813a02dbf5057: Now you're getting it! While it doesn't seem to be innately aggressive, our concern is the potential risk of it tunnelling into Cam Torum.
